intro.leadQuoteTemplate: Riomaggiore's weather is more than just a forecast; it's the heartbeat of the village. Today, the atmosphere is defined by {condition} skies and a crisp {temp}°C.
intro.paragraph: They are presented as they deserve to be: with care, context, and a human voice.

giuliaSection.title: Giulia's Weather Journal
giuliaSection.signatureQuote: Watch the horizon—it always tells the truth first.

closing.title: Watch the Horizon
closing.paragraph: Coastal weather is a living thing. It changes with the tide, the wind, and the season. Plan your day, but be ready to let the village surprise you. The best experiences in Riomaggiore are often the ones you didn't plan.

giuliaAdvice.note: December in Riomaggiore has a specific kind of light—crisp and clear. It's not beach weather, but it's the best time for the high trails. The air is fresh, and you won't break a sweat climbing to Montenero. Just remember: the sun drops behind the cliffs early, so the village gets chilly by 4 PM. Carry a light wool scarf—it's the local way.
recommendations: Perfect for the hike to Santuario di Montenero.
Ideal light for photography in the harbor around 3 PM.
Grab a hot 'Sciacchetrà' tasting at a local enoteca as the sun sets.
